article_title: Huawei Launches Pura X Max, the First Wide-Format Foldable, Beating Samsung and Apple to a New Smartphone Category
summary: Multiple Pura X Max hardware specs and pricing not supported by cited sources; aspect ratio claim contradicts 9to5Google

findings.details: Bot claims 'aspect ratio close to 2:1' for the Pura X Max inner display. The 9to5Google snapshot explicitly states 'the inner display aspect ratio is somewhere around 16:11' (~1.45:1, not 2:1). The summary also calls it 'roughly 2:1 paper-shaped'.

findings.message: Pura X Max hardware specs not in cited sources
findings.details: Bot attributes the following claims to 9to5Google but they are NOT in the snapshot: '7.7-inch internal LTPO OLED display', '5.4-inch external screen', 'both panels running at up to 120Hz'. The 9to5Google source explicitly says 'The exact specs aren't shown on Huawei's early listing'.

findings.message: Pura X Max starting price not in cited sources
findings.details: Bot attributes '10,999 yuan, or approximately $1,610, for the 12GB/256GB configuration' to 9to5Google. 9to5Google lists the storage configurations (12GB/256GB through 16GB/1TB) but does NOT provide a starting price. SCMP and CNBC do not cover the Pura X Max launch.

content_preview.title: Huawei Launches Pura X Max, the First Wide-Format Foldable, Beating Samsung and Apple to a New Smartphone Category
content_preview.summary: Huawei unveiled the Pura X Max on April 20 in Guangzhou, the first foldable smartphone to open horizontally into a roughly 2:1 paper-shaped display, undercutting expected wide-fold launches from Samsung and Apple later this year.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Huawei launched the Pura X Max on April 20, 2026 at a Guangzhou product event, positioning the device as the industry's first horizontally wide foldable smartphone. Unlike the book-style foldables that have defined the category since 2019, the Pura X Max opens into a near-square 7.7-inch display rather than a tall rectangle, a form factor [9to5Google described](https://9to5google.com/2026/04/13/huawei-pura-x-max-wide-foldable/) as kicking off the wide foldable trend ahead of competi...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article (903 words). Strong context on the original Pura X and broader market dynamics. Critical issue: several Pura X Max hardware specs and pricing are presented as factual but not supported by any cited source.

source_verification.https://9to5google.com/2026/04/13/huawei: source-0.html — Confirmed: April 20 launch, wide form factor, 12GB/256GB to 16GB/1TB RAM/storage configs, five colors (Interstellar Blue, Olive Gold, Phantom Night Black, Zero Degree White, Vibrant Orange), 'inner display aspect ratio is somewhere around 16:11' (NOT 2:1 as bot claims), Samsung/Apple wide-foldable competitive context, China-only availability, possible Kirin 9030. CRITICAL: '7.7-inch internal LTPO OLED', '5.4-inch external', '120Hz', '10,999 yuan starting price' are NOT in this snapshot. The source explicitly says 'The exact specs aren't shown on Huawei's early listing'.
source_verification.https://www.scmp.com/tech/tech-trends/ar: source-1.html — Verified: 800 yuan price cut on the Pura X (the original), Pura X has 16:10 aspect on 6.3-inch display, ~70% Q3 2025 Chinese foldable share per IDC, Samsung/Apple ~4:3 wide foldables expected per ETnews and supply-chain tipsters. Does not cover Pura X Max specs.
source_verification.https://www.cnbc.com/2025/03/20/huawei-p: source-2.html — Verified verbatim: original Pura X 7,499 yuan / $1,037 starting price, 6.3-inch 16:10 display, first to ship with HarmonyOS 5 (HarmonyOS Next), Xiaoyi AI assistant with Huawei + DeepSeek models, Q4 2024 China share Huawei 13.7→16.2%, Apple 20→17.4%. This is March 2025 launch coverage of the original Pura X — does NOT cover the Pura X Max.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Original Pura X claims (7,499 yuan, 6.3-inch 16:10, HarmonyOS 5, Xiaoyi+DeepSeek, Q4 2024 market share) all check out. Pura X Max launch date (April 20), wide form factor, RAM/storage options, five colors, China-only release also check out. PROBLEMATIC: 7.7-inch LTPO OLED inner panel, 5.4-inch external panel, 120Hz, 10,999 yuan starting price, and 'close to 2:1' aspect ratio are NOT in any cited source. The 9to5Google source the bot attributes them to explicitly says specs are unconfirmed. The aspect ratio in 9to5Google is '16:11', not '2:1'.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical. The 'wide foldable category' framing is reasonable. Setup of the original Pura X / Pura X Max relationship is well done.
editor_notes.originality: Not previously covered. Builds appropriately on the Huawei Pura X market context.
concerns: Bot states '7.7-inch internal LTPO OLED display ... 5.4-inch external screen, with both panels running at up to 120Hz' attributed to 9to5Google. None of these specs appear in the 9to5Google snapshot, which says the official specs have not yet been shared.
Bot states pricing 'starts at 10,999 yuan, or approximately $1,610, for the 12GB/256GB configuration' attributed to 9to5Google. The starting price is not in any cited source.
Bot states the Pura X Max 'internal panel uses an aspect ratio close to 2:1' but the 9to5Google source says ~16:11 (~1.45:1). The summary repeats the 'roughly 2:1 paper-shaped display' claim.
recommendations: Either re-source the hardware specs (7.7-inch, 5.4-inch, 120Hz, LTPO OLED) and starting price from a source that actually carries them (e.g., GSMArena, Huawei's official listing, or a hands-on review) — or remove these specifics and frame the spec section as 'specs not yet officially confirmed' as 9to5Google does.
Correct the aspect ratio: 9to5Google says ~16:11, not ~2:1. Update both the body and the summary.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REQUEST_CHANGES. The article's framing of the launch and context on the original Pura X are accurate, but multiple presented-as-factual claims about the Pura X Max's hardware specs, refresh rate, panel technology, starting price, and aspect ratio do not appear in any cited source — and the 9to5Google source explicitly says official specs haven't been shared. The bot must either re-source these claims or remove them.
